steps.title: Identify why the researchers say labels are temporary
steps.content:
The researchers' core claim is that “invasive” and “native” are labels that describe temporary circumstances — temporary because climate change will shift where animals can live.

The passage then applies this specifically to Maryland: if climate change will alter conditions there, then current classifications based on today's conditions may not fit tomorrow's conditions.

steps.title: Match this logic to the choices
steps.content:
Choice C directly states that Maryland's current designation of flathead catfish as invasive may be appropriate now but not in the future — exactly the time-bound shift the researchers' argument predicts.

Choice A contradicts the logic by excluding flathead catfish from the “temporary circumstances” principle that applies to all species.

Choice B reverses the timeline, suggesting past presence rather than future change.

Choice D undermines the entire argument by claiming the catfish will establish itself regardless of climate predictions, when the researchers' point depends on climate change as the mechanism for range shifts.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
